headline: Risk-on returns: Wall Street reopened to a record Dow and 'revived AI optimism', and India's IT stocks led a fifth straight up-day ahead of TCS's results — while oil bounced off oversold lows and the rupee steadied near ~95.3.
market_story: The mood flipped back to risk-on. When US markets reopened after the July-4th weekend, the story that had dominated last week — money fleeing expensive AI-chip names — reversed: 'revived AI optimism' lifted technology broadly, sending the Dow to a record close of ~53,056 (+0.29%), the S&P 500 up 0.72% to ~7,537 and the Nasdaq up 1.12% to ~26,121. That risk appetite carried into India, where the Nifty and Sensex traded modestly higher again on Tuesday — a fifth straight up-session — but with the leadership rotating: yesterday banks drove the rally on cheap oil; today it was information-technology (IT services firms that build software for global clients and earn in dollars), with Infosys up ~3.5% and TCS ~1.9% as investors positioned ahead of TCS's Q1 results on 9 July. The macro backdrop stayed friendly: the 'fear gauge' VIX sank ~3.6% into the mid-15s (calm, lightly-hedged markets), gold slipped ~0.4% to ~$4,139 as safe-haven demand cooled, and crude oil bounced 1.3% to ~$69.4 — still technically deeply oversold (RSI ~31), meaning it has fallen so fast a rebound was due, but not enough to threaten India's cheap-fuel tailwind. The rupee steadied around 95.3 per dollar, just off last week's record low, with Wednesday's first Fed minutes under new chair Kevin Warsh the next swing factor. Among your favorites the split is stark: Apple ($312.66, +1.31%) kept extending its breakout as the AI-optimism session held its foldable-iPhone pop; Nvidia ($195.55, +0.37%) barely moved and stayed a laggard even on a tech-up day, still rated Strong Sell just above the $191 line that defines its trend; Reliance (₹1,314.9, -0.48%) gave back a little in a still-intact downtrend; and Bitcoin (~$63,291, -1.1% on the day) has clawed back above the ~$62.4k support it broke yesterday, but the bigger downtrend is unchanged.

board.name: S&P 500
board.read: US large-caps rose 0.72% at Monday's close on revived AI optimism — the broad market confirming the tech-led bounce, back near record territory.

board.name: Nasdaq
board.read: The tech-heavy index led (+1.12%) as money rotated BACK into AI names it fled last week — last week's laggard became this week's leader.

board.name: Dow Jones
board.read: A record close above 53k — but RSI ~71 says it's short-term 'overbought' (stretched after a fast run), so a pause would be normal. Newly includes Alphabet.

board.name: VIX (volatility)
board.read: Wall Street's 'fear gauge' fell to the mid-15s — calm, lightly-hedged markets. Low VIX is comfort now but little cushion if a shock lands.

board.name: Nifty 50
board.read: India's benchmark edged higher again intraday (fifth straight up-day), this time led by IT rather than banks, ahead of TCS earnings on Thursday.

board.name: Sensex
board.read: The 30-stock index tracked higher intraday — a steady, broad up-move with IT and oil & gas buying offsetting weakness in power and metals.

board.name: Bank Nifty
board.read: Banks paused after leading yesterday (RSI ~65, above key averages) — the baton passed to IT as the market rotated ahead of results.

board.name: Bitcoin
board.read: Choppy: down 1.1% on the day but back above the ~$62.4k support it broke yesterday. Still below its 50- and 200-day averages (a downtrend). Support ~$62,632, resistance ~$64,086.

board.name: Ether
board.read: Ethereum eased with Bitcoin (-1.02% to ~$1,779) — technically still weak, below both key moving averages.

board.name: Gold
board.read: Softened ~0.4% to ~$4,139 as risk-on returns and safe-haven demand cools — the mirror image of the equity bounce.

board.name: Crude Oil (WTI)
board.read: Bounced 1.3% off deeply oversold levels (RSI ~31) but still sub-$70 — a technical rebound, not a trend change. Cheap oil remains India's tailwind.

board.name: USD / INR
board.read: The rupee steadied near 95.3, just off last week's record low — a firm dollar caps any recovery until Wednesday's Fed minutes give direction.

themes.note: The dominant flip: 'revived AI optimism' reversed last week's rotation OUT of AI-chip names — Nasdaq +1.12%, Dow to a record. Note the divergence: Apple extended, but Nvidia barely participated and stays technically weak, so it's optimism rewarding some names more than others.

Leadership rotated from banks to IT ahead of earnings: Infosys ~+3.5%, TCS ~+1.9%, Tech Mahindra higher. A weaker rupee helps these dollar-earners, and investors are positioning before TCS opens the season Thursday (~Jul 9).
VIX down ~3.6% to the mid-15s and gold softer ~0.4% — classic risk-on: fear and safe-havens ease as stocks rally. The flip side is complacency: few are hedged if a surprise lands.

Crude bounced 1.3% to ~$69.4 off a deeply oversold RSI ~31 — a technical rebound after OPEC+'s August hike and the Hormuz reopening drove it down. Still sub-$70, so India's cheap-fuel tailwind is intact; watch for an Iran/Hormuz headline that revives a war premium.

Weak June jobs (57k) killed hike fear but sticky ~4.2% inflation blocks cuts — 'higher for longer'. Wednesday's June-meeting minutes, the first under new chair Kevin Warsh, are the week's read on how the new Fed thinks.

what_to_watch.title: TCS opens India's earnings season Thursday — IT is already front-running it
key_points: TCS reports Q1 (Apr–Jun) results on 9 July, with a possible dividend
IT led today: Infosys ~+3.5%, TCS ~+1.9%
The sector has been under an 'AI will disrupt us' cloud all year
A weaker rupee (~95.3) quietly lifts these dollar-earners
what_to_watch.gist: Indian IT stocks led the market on Tuesday — Infosys up about 3.5%, TCS about 1.9% — as investors positioned two days before TCS opens the April–June (Q1 FY27) results season on 9 July. It's a bet that the sector, clouded all year by fears AI will erode its outsourcing business, will deliver reassuring numbers and orders.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: IT is a heavyweight in the Nifty and Sensex, so its results swing the whole index. Because the market has already rallied INTO the print, the risk cuts both ways: strong numbers and upbeat AI-demand commentary validate the move and could pull back foreign sellers; a miss hits IT hardest and punctures the 'India as calm harbour' story fastest.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For an Indian investor this is the fortnight the rally gets audited. Watch TCS's revenue growth, deal wins and management tone on AI (threat vs. demand driver), then Infosys and the rest. A weaker rupee is a tailwind — IT earns in dollars, so each dollar converts to more rupees.
what_to_watch.watch: TCS Q1 results and commentary (~9 July); whether the Nifty holds above ~24,450 and extends, or sells the news.

key_terms.definition: India's first fiscal quarter — April to June 2026; Indian firms report on an April–March financial year.
Buying a stock or sector in anticipation of good news before it's announced, so the price has already moved by the time results land.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: Markets price the future, so a sector often moves before its news — which means the reaction to results depends on expectations already built in: a good number can still disappoint if the stock had run up in anticipation.

what_to_watch.title: The new Fed chair's first minutes land Wednesday — the dollar and rupee hinge on it
key_points: June Fed meeting minutes released Wednesday (Jul 8, US time)
First primary document under new chair Kevin Warsh
Weak jobs (57k) + sticky ~4.2% inflation = Fed 'stuck'
A firm dollar keeps the rupee pinned near ~95.3
what_to_watch.gist: Wednesday brings the minutes — the detailed written record — of the June Federal Reserve meeting, the first under new chair Kevin Warsh. With June hiring weak (57,000 jobs) but inflation still sticky near 4.2%, the Fed is 'stuck': no hikes to fear, but no cuts either. The minutes are the first real window into how this committee reasons.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: With a new chair and no track record, markets are re-learning the Fed's reaction function from scratch, so early documents move prices more than usual. A hawkish (inflation-first) tone firms the dollar and pressures rate-sensitive assets; an acknowledgement of the cooling jobs market nudges 'no hike' toward 'maybe cuts', helping stocks and gold. It recalibrates the July 29 rate decision.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For an Indian investor the channel is the dollar and foreign flows: a hawkish read keeps the rupee near 95.3 and foreign money cautious; a softer read eases both and could lift Indian equities. Crypto, the purest rates-and-liquidity trade, will react first.
what_to_watch.watch: Wednesday's Fed minutes tone; the dollar and rupee response; the July 29 rate decision.

key_terms.definition: The written record of a Federal Reserve meeting, released weeks later — traders parse it for clues the short statement didn't give.
A central bank keeping rates elevated for an extended period because inflation stays stubborn, which tends to strengthen its currency.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: When leadership changes at a central bank, its first words carry outsized weight — markets have no history to lean on, so they over-react to any hint of the new chair's leanings.

what_to_watch.title: AI optimism snaps back — but Nvidia sits out the rally
key_points: Nasdaq +1.12%, Dow to a record on 'revived AI optimism'
Nvidia barely moved (+0.37%) even on a tech-up day
It's still rated Strong Sell, just above its $191 200-day line
Apple, by contrast, extended its breakout (+1.31%)
what_to_watch.gist: The AI trade reversed last week's selloff: technology led Monday's US session, lifting the Nasdaq 1.12% and the Dow to a record. But the bounce was selective — Apple extended its run, while Nvidia gained only 0.37% and remains technically weak, rated Strong Sell and sitting just ~$4 above its 200-day average (~$191), the line that separates 'a pullback' from 'a broken trend'.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: The divergence is the signal. 'Revived AI optimism' is rewarding some names (software, cloud, Apple's hardware story) more than the chipmaker that was the poster child of the boom — a sign investors are getting more selective about where the AI payoff actually lands. That selectivity, not blanket enthusiasm, is the healthier but choppier phase of a theme.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For a holder, watch whether Nvidia can hold ~$191 and reclaim its 50-day (~$210) — until it does, it's a laggard even on up days. For Indian IT, the read-through is positive: reviving AI optimism generally lifts sentiment toward the whole tech complex.
what_to_watch.watch: Nvidia's $191 (200-day) support and $210 (50-day) resistance; next AI-chip and cloud earnings and capex guidance.

key_terms.definition: The average closing price over the last 200 trading days — a widely-watched line dividing a long-term uptrend (price above) from a downtrend (price below).
Money moving between sectors or names; here, back into AI broadly but selectively, favouring some names over others.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: A sector rally is rarely uniform — when a theme revives, watch which names DON'T participate: relative weakness in a former leader (like Nvidia) is often an early warning that the market's conviction has narrowed.

what_to_watch.title: Oil bounces off oversold — but the cheap-fuel tailwind for India holds
key_points: WTI rose 1.3% to ~$69.4 after a deep selloff (RSI ~31)
Still sub-$70 — a technical rebound, not a trend change
OPEC+'s August hike and Hormuz reopening drove it down
An Iran/Hormuz headline could revive a war premium
what_to_watch.gist: Crude oil bounced 1.3% to about $69.4 (WTI), a rebound off deeply oversold levels — its RSI near 31 signalled it had fallen so fast and far that a bounce was overdue. The drop was driven by OPEC+ agreeing to pump more from August and the Strait of Hormuz reopening; today's move is a technical breather, not a reversal.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: Oil is India's biggest import, so its price is a hidden hand on the whole market: cheap crude eases fuel costs, inflation and the import bill, giving the RBI room on rates — the fuel behind India's four-day bank rally. A bounce off oversold is normal and doesn't change that; only a sustained move back above the low-$70s would start to erode the tailwind.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: Sub-$70 oil remains a quiet daily gift to India — supportive for banks, consumers and oil-consuming sectors, and for Reliance's refining feedstock costs. The risk is a supply-shock reversal: an Iran flare-up around Wednesday's Khamenei burial or a Hormuz 'fees' clash would put the war premium straight back.
what_to_watch.watch: Whether WTI holds the high-$60s or breaks back above the low-$70s; any Iran/Hormuz headline around the 9 July burial.

key_terms.definition: A momentum reading near/below 30 suggesting a price has fallen so fast a bounce is likely — a caution flag against chasing the drop, not a buy signal by itself.
Extra money added to a commodity's price because conflict threatens supply, even before any actual shortage.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: 'Oversold' means a fall was fast, not that it's over — bounces off extreme readings are normal and can happen inside an ongoing downtrend, so one green day doesn't reverse the bigger move.

what_to_watch.title: Bitcoin reclaims the level it broke — but the downtrend is unchanged
key_points: Bitcoin ~$63,291, down 1.1% on the day but above yesterday's ~$61.9k
Back above the ~$62.4k support it broke on Monday
Still below its 50-day (~$66.2k) and 200-day (~$74.5k) averages
Resistance ~$64,086; support ~$62,632
what_to_watch.gist: Bitcoin is choppy: down about 1.1% over the day to ~$63,291, but net higher than yesterday's ~$61,908 close — it has clawed back above the ~$62.4k support it lost on Monday. It remains in a confirmed downtrend (below its 50-day ~$66.2k and 200-day ~$74.5k averages) with strong downward trend strength (ADX ~28), near the bottom tenth of its 52-week range.
what_to_watch.why_it_matters: Reclaiming a broken support is a small positive, but nothing about the bigger downtrend has changed — the trend-defining averages are still overhead. Because crypto trades 24/7 with no earnings, it's the purest read on risk appetite and liquidity, so its wobble even on a risk-on equity day is a note of caution.
what_to_watch.what_it_means: For a holder, the ~$66.2k 50-day average is still the level that would signal a genuine turn; below it, bounces are relief rallies inside a downtrend. Short term, watch the ~$62,632 support and Wednesday's Fed-minutes tone.
what_to_watch.watch: The ~$62,632 support and ~$64,086 resistance; a real turn needs a reclaim of the ~$66.2k 50-day average; Wednesday's Fed minutes.

key_terms.definition: Price levels where buying (support) or selling (resistance) has repeatedly clustered; breaking and reclaiming them signals shifts in the balance of the two.
A gauge of trend STRENGTH (not direction); a high ADX (~28) means the current move — here, down — has real momentum behind it.

what_to_watch.the_lesson: Reclaiming a broken level is encouraging but not decisive — inside a confirmed downtrend, the longer moving averages matter more than any single support, and bounces should be treated as relief until those averages are retaken.

global_summary.us: US markets reopened after the July-4th holiday with a risk-on session: the Dow closed at a record ~53,056 (+0.29%), the S&P 500 rose 0.72% to ~7,537 and the Nasdaq gained 1.12% to ~26,121 on 'revived AI optimism' that reversed last week's AI-chip selloff. The VIX sank to the mid-15s. The week's main event is Wednesday's June Fed minutes — the first under new chair Kevin Warsh.
global_summary.india: Indian markets edged higher again intraday — a fifth straight up-day — with the Nifty above ~24,450 and the Sensex up ~0.3%, led this time by IT (Infosys ~+3.5%, TCS ~+1.9%) rather than banks, as oil & gas firmed and power/metals lagged. The rupee steadied near ~95.3. The next catalysts: TCS Q1 results (~Jul 9), the Adani filing (Jul 13) and the India–UK trade pact (Jul 15).
global_summary.crypto: Bitcoin is choppy near ~$63,291 (-1.1% on the day but back above the ~$62.4k support it broke Monday); Ether ~$1,779 (-1.02%). Both remain below their 50- and 200-day averages — relief bounces inside a downtrend.
global_summary.macro: Crude bounced 1.3% off oversold to ~$69.4 (WTI) but stays sub-$70 — India's cheap-fuel tailwind intact; gold softened ~0.4% to ~$4,139 as risk-on returned; the rupee steadied ~95.3. Key dates: NATO summit Jul 7–8, Le Pen verdict Jul 7, Fed minutes Jul 8 (US), Khamenei burial Jul 9, Fed decision Jul 29.

catalysts_today.event: Indian markets edge higher a 5th straight day, IT-led (Infosys ~+3.5%, TCS ~+1.9%) ahead of earnings
US reopened to a record Dow (~53,056) and broad tech rally on 'revived AI optimism'
Paris appeals-court verdict on Marine Le Pen's eligibility (French bonds/euro)
NATO summit opens in Ankara (Jul 7–8); ~€70bn Ukraine aid pledge; Trump–Zelensky meeting Wednesday
Modi signs India–Indonesia BrahMos-missile pact in Jakarta; then Australia summit Jul 8–10
June Fed meeting minutes — first under new chair Kevin Warsh
TCS Q1 FY27 results — opens India's earnings season
US DoJ deadline to justify permanently dismissing the Adani case
India–UK free-trade pact (CETA) comes into force — 99% of Indian exports duty-free

favorites.name: Apple Inc.
favorites.whats_new: Apple's breakout held and extended: on the US reopen it added another 1.31% to $312.66, building on last week's foldable-iPhone/cheaper-memory-chip surge. It's the strongest chart in the favorites book — a confirmed uptrend with all 10 moving averages bullish, sitting at the 98th percentile of its 52-week range. RSI ~62 still has room, but short-term gauges (stochastic, CCI, Williams %R) all flag 'overbought', so a pause near the ~$314 resistance wouldn't surprise. It's the clearest winner of the 'revived AI optimism' session.

news.headline: Stock market today: Dow posts record, S&P 500 and Nasdaq rally on revived AI optimism

favorites.fundamentals: Mega-cap tech. Trailing P/E on the rich side (you're paying up for growth), forward P/E lower (profits expected to grow ~mid-teens). Keeps roughly 27¢ of every sales dollar as profit; beta ~1.1 (about as jumpy as the market). Priced near record highs, so it needs the growth story to keep delivering.
favorites.technicals: Strong Buy. Price ($312.66) is above both its 50-day (~$294) and 200-day (~$271) averages — the classic healthy-uptrend shape — at the 98th percentile of its 52-week range. RSI ~62 is neutral (room to run), MACD bullish, but stochastic/CCI/Williams %R are overbought, so short-term chop is likely. Resistance ~$314 then higher; a close below the 50-day (~$294) would be the first warning the trend is stalling.
favorites.catalysts: Confirmation of foldable-iPhone component orders; next quarterly earnings and AI/services commentary; broad tech risk appetite.
favorites.risks: Rich valuation leaves no room for a miss, and it's priced near record highs on reports rather than confirmed numbers — a broader tech pullback or a fade in the AI-optimism mood could drag it despite its own strength; short-term overbought signals warn of a near-term breather.

favorites.name: Reliance Industries
favorites.whats_new: Reliance gave back a little, slipping 0.48% to ₹1,314.9 after yesterday's bounce, as oil ticked up off its lows (a mild negative for a refiner's crack margins) and the market's energy leadership faded to IT. It's still below both its 50-day (~₹1,341) and 200-day (~₹1,418) averages — the longer downtrend is intact — though MACD momentum is quietly improving and it sits at just the 17th percentile of its 52-week range, where value hunters watch. The real catalyst is its own Q1 results later this season.

news.headline: Sensex Today Trades Higher; Nifty Above 24,450; Infosys & Tech Mahindra Top Gainers
Brent slips below $72, WTI near $68.5 as OPEC+ approves August output hike

favorites.fundamentals: Mega-cap, energy-heavy conglomerate (refining, petrochemicals, Jio telecom, retail). Trailing P/E ~22 / forward ~18 (moderate), revenue +12% a year, ~8¢ of every sales rupee kept as profit; 0.5% dividend yield. Beta ~0.18 — far steadier than the market.
favorites.technicals: Neutral rating within a weak downtrend (ADX ~15). Price (₹1,314.9) is below the 50-day (~₹1,341) and 200-day (~₹1,418) averages, at the 17th percentile of its 52-week range. RSI ~50 (neutral), MACD improving. Reclaiming ~₹1,341 (50-day) is what would flip the near-term trend; first resistance ~₹1,325, first support ~₹1,307.
favorites.catalysts: Q1 FY27 results this season (refining margins on cheap-but-steady crude; Jio and retail momentum); any India-market re-rating on the earnings/trade-pact/Adani trio.
favorites.risks: The longer downtrend holds until ₹1,341 is reclaimed; an oil rebound squeezes refining economics, and a weak Q1 would stall the recovery. A high dollar/soft rupee is a mixed factor for the group.

favorites.name: Nvidia
favorites.whats_new: The tell of the day: on a session of 'revived AI optimism' that lifted the whole tech complex, Nvidia managed only +0.37% to $195.55 — a laggard even when its own theme rallied. It held just above its 200-day average (~$191), the line that keeps the long-term uptrend alive, but stayed below its 50-day (~$210) and is still rated Strong Sell with 8 of 10 moving averages bearish and MACD momentum negative. Relative weakness while peers rally is the signal to watch.

news.headline: Stock market today: Dow posts record, S&P 500 and Nasdaq rally on revived AI optimism
AI is 'not smart' — what's next in artificial intelligence?

favorites.fundamentals: Mega-cap with exceptional ~63¢-per-dollar profit margins. Trailing P/E ~30, forward P/E ~15 — the market still expects profits to compound fast (revenue +85% a year). Beta ~2.21: it swings roughly twice as hard as the market, both ways.
favorites.technicals: Strong Sell (short-term). Price ($195.55) is below its 50-day (~$210) but above its 200-day (~$191) — a transitional zone that breaks one way or the other. RSI ~42 is weak but not oversold; MACD negative. The line in the sand: hold ~$191 (200-day) and the long-term uptrend survives; close below it and the trend is broken. Reclaiming ~$210 would be the first genuine repair signal. Resistance ~$197, support ~$194.
favorites.catalysts: Whether it can reclaim the ~$210 50-day; next earnings and cloud-capex guidance — the real referendum on AI spending.
favorites.risks: A high-beta name that failed to participate in an AI-up day — if optimism narrows further or the ~$191 200-day breaks, drawdowns can be fast. Watch Typhoon Bavi's Taiwan track (~Jul 11) as a tail-risk for chip supply chains.

favorites.name: Bitcoin
favorites.whats_new: Bitcoin clawed back the level it lost: after slipping below ~$62.4k support on Monday, it has recovered to ~$63,291 — down 1.1% on the day but net higher than yesterday's ~$61,908 close. That reclaim is a small positive, but the ruling trend is unchanged: still below the 50-day (~$66.2k) and 200-day (~$74.5k) averages, with strong downward trend strength (ADX ~28) and price near the bottom tenth of its 52-week range. A relief bounce, not a turn.

news.headline: Current price of Bitcoin for July 6, 2026
Bitcoin and ethereum prices today: prices falling after last week's rebound

favorites.fundamentals: ~$1.25T total market value; sits near the bottom tenth (9th percentile) of its 52-week range after the slide from ~$113k. No cash flows — price is set by flows, halving-cycle sentiment and risk appetite.
favorites.technicals: Neutral rating, weak trend. Price (~$63,291) is below both the 50-day (~$66,212) and 200-day (~$74,482) averages — a confirmed downtrend with strong trend strength (ADX ~28) — RSI ~50 (neutral), MACD improving. Support ~$62,632; a real turn needs a reclaim of ~$64,086, then the ~$66.2k 50-day. Lose $62,632 and the recent lows reopen.
favorites.catalysts: Wednesday's Fed-minutes tone, ETF flow data, and the broad risk-on/off read — crypto is the purest rates-and-liquidity trade on the board.
favorites.risks: Downtrend intact and the 50-day still overhead; a slip below ~$62,632 invites momentum selling. As the 24/7 risk gauge, its wobble even on a risk-on equity day is a note of caution.

risks: Complacency into a risk-on tape: with the VIX in the mid-15s and gold softening, few are hedged — any negative surprise (a hawkish Fed read, a NATO-summit failure, an Iran/Hormuz flare-up around the Jul 9 burial) lands on an unprepared market.
The Fed minutes are an unknown quantity: a hawkish first document from new chair Warsh could firm the dollar, pressure the ~95.3 rupee and unwind the Dow's overbought record run.
India earnings risk: a five-day rally now rests on IT strength priced INTO the print — a weak or cautious TCS result Thursday would hit IT hardest and puncture the 'India as calm harbour' narrative.
The AI bounce is narrow: Nvidia failed to join a tech-up day and sits ~$4 above its 200-day — a break of ~$191 would ripple through the Nasdaq, high-beta tech and Indian IT sentiment.
Crypto trend still down: Bitcoin only reclaimed the level it broke; a slip back below ~$62,632 would reopen the recent lows and dent broader risk appetite.

Oil could snap back: crude is bouncing off oversold (WTI RSI ~31), so an Iran/Hormuz headline or Ukraine escalation could quickly restore a war premium — reversing India's main tailwind. Typhoon Bavi's forecast track toward Taiwan (~Jul 11) is a separate chip-supply tail-risk.
disclaimer: For information only. Not financial advice. Prices are from free, possibly-delayed sources (yfinance/CoinGecko); Indian levels are intraday, US levels are the last close. Verify before acting.
